evals.prompt: Create a portfolio case study presentation for my Group Ordering project. Context: I was the lead PM. We unlocked B2B and office ordering on a food delivery platform. Results: AOV went from €28 to €44 (+57%), revenue per order up 83%, margin per order up 166%. I want a cover, a context slide explaining the situation, a slide on what we built, a results slide with the metrics, a learnings slide, and a closing slide. My name is Helmut Fritz.
evals.expected_output: A Google Slides presentation cloned from the personal template with 6-8 slides: cover with the project name and Helmut Fritz, context/situation slide, what-we-built content slide, a metrics/results slide featuring the key numbers, a learnings slide, and a closing. Brand voice is direct and evidence-led. Returns a shareable link.
expectations: The presentation is created by cloning template 1f1t52kxB3satmCRLo1PxY4wywZSKFT4T5Np0t9WsOfo
The cover slide includes 'Group Ordering' as the title and 'Helmut Fritz' as the presenter
A context or situation slide explains the problem/opportunity
A results slide prominently features the three key metrics: AOV +57% (€28→€44), Revenue/order +83%, Margin/order +166%
A learnings or retrospective slide is included
A closing slide is included
Slide titles state conclusions, not topics (e.g. 'AOV grew 57% within 3 months' not 'Results')
All text uses sentence case — only first word and proper nouns capitalised
Active voice throughout: 'we built', 'the team shipped', not 'was built by'
A shareable Google Slides URL is returned

evals.prompt: Build me a 10-slide strategy pitch deck. The idea: I want to pitch a new product intelligence service for mid-size e-commerce companies. Problem: most mid-size e-commerce teams can't afford a full product analytics team, so they make gut-feel decisions. Solution: a lightweight AI-powered product analytics tool that gives weekly 'what happened and why' reports automatically. Target: 50–500 person e-commerce companies. Pricing: €299/month. I need a problem slide, solution slide, how it works, target market, competitive landscape, pricing, go-to-market plan, expected traction, and a closing with my contact.
evals.expected_output: A 10-11 slide pitch deck with a strong narrative arc: problem → solution → how it works → market → competition → pricing → go-to-market → traction → close. Direct, confident voice. Key metrics and comparisons where relevant. Returns a shareable link.
expectations: The presentation is created by cloning the personal template
A cover slide names the product concept and Helmut Fritz
A problem slide leads with a concrete statement about the pain (not a vague topic header)
A solution slide describes the product offering clearly
A 'how it works' or product walkthrough slide is included
A target market or ICP slide is included with the 50-500 person e-commerce framing
A competitive landscape or positioning slide is included
A pricing slide shows the €299/month model
A go-to-market or traction slide is included
A closing slide includes contact info (helmut.fritz.v@gmail.com)
Slide titles are conclusions, not topics
All text uses sentence case
A shareable Google Slides URL is returned

evals.prompt: Quick 5-slide project update deck for a client. Project: onboarding flow redesign for a B2B SaaS product. Status: green. Completed: user research (12 interviews done), wireframes approved. In progress: frontend build (40% done). Blocked: waiting on final copy from the client's content team. Next steps: finish build by June 20, QA June 21-25, launch June 27. Keep it tight.
evals.expected_output: A concise 5-slide deck: cover, status overview (RAG green), completed work, in-progress + blockers, next steps / timeline. Tight and scannable copy. Returns a shareable link.
expectations: The presentation is created by cloning the personal template
Cover slide names the project and Helmut Fritz
A status slide communicates the green RAG status clearly
Completed work (research + wireframes) is on a separate slide from next steps
The blocker (client content team) is called out explicitly
A next steps or timeline slide includes the key dates (June 20, 25, 27)
The deck is approximately 5 slides — not bloated
All text uses sentence case
A shareable Google Slides URL is returned
